questions.q: Bert: Can I have a word with you, Professor Welgrow? It’s about my career plan. Professor Welgow: Sure! What do you have in mind? Professor Welgow: Well, After all, you’re still a sophomore, and you still have plenty of time to decide. Bert: I think you’re right. Thanks a lot.
options: I’d advise you to explore more possibilities first.

questions.q: Neddy: Can you believe that I had to work every weekend last month? That’s it! I’m quitting my job! Ken: Come on, pal! I know you’re stressed out, First of all, let me ask you something: you honestly think you can find a better job than this one you have now? Neddy: I suppose not. Ken: So stop talking nonsense about quitting!
options: so I wouldn’t be here listening to you complain.
for such a thing happens to them from time to time.
but that’s no excuse for doing something so silly.

and I’m telling you that I feel the same way, too. III. 綜合測驗 說明:以下兩篇短文,共有 10 個空格,為第 21 至 30 題,每題有四個選項,請依各篇短文文意, 選出一個最適合該空格的答案。 ▲ 下篇短文共有 5 個空格,為第 21-25 題,請依短文文意,選出一個最適合該空格的答案。 Nowadays, most parents would like their children to learn how to swim. As far as they are concerned, swimming is 21 a practical skill 21 a wonderful form of exercise. Usually we tend to picture a standard swimming lesson as being like this: the coach first asks each student to put on his or her swimming suit, do some warm-ups, and then jump into the swimming pool. Afterwards, the coach will 22 teaching the students different swimming techniques. In contrast to the above, children in Holland learn to swim in a rather 23 way. Instead of putting on their swimming suits, they are trained to swim 24 their clothes on. As for why they teach their children this way, experts in Holland argue that for their children, learning to swim is a necessity, 25 main purpose is to save their lives if they drop into the water. And when that happens, they can’t possibly have any time to put on their swimming suits. Hence, they must learn how to swim while they’re still wearing their clothes!
questions.explanation: 納豆:你相信我上個月每個週末都得工作嗎?到此為 是肥胖,或是糖尿病等等。到那時候,我們或許就會說: 止!這工作我不幹了! 「早知道當初 (30) 就不要吃甜食來解決!」 阿 Ken:別這樣,兄弟!我知道你壓力大,

questions.subject: 英文
questions.unit: 110-4-四技二專-00共同科目 英文
questions.q: According to the above, which of the following is NOT TRUE?
options: Walt ordered a 12:30 ticket for The Cold Case online, so he needed to get to Hollywood Cineplex by noon.
It will cost Jay 8 dollars in all if he wants to see The Cold Case at 10:20 and have some popcorn as well.
Spending $24, Jewel and Flora could enjoy their hot dogs and cokes while watching Worms at 18:30.

Candy would like to refund her 20:20 ticket for Worms, and she needed to get it done by 20:10. ▲ 閱讀下文,回答第 33-37 題 Having graduated from a drama school, Sid is now an outstanding actor. While he’s not acting, he is working as an engineer because he also has an M.(A) degree in engineering. In his case, Sid is an actor / engineer (, which reads, “Sid is an actor ‘slash’ engineer.”) Recently, the word “slash” has made its way into the local Chinese language in Taiwan. It may be used as a verb (People in Taiwan may say, “Sid has been ‘slashing’ for quite a while.”) or an adjective (People in Taiwan may also say, “Sid has had a ‘slash’ life”). “Slashing” or being “slash,” in fact, is a new, trendy concept that has many deep meanings. First of all, it is not just about working at two jobs. (That is, if a young man waits on tables in the day and works in a convenience store at night, he’s not exactly “slashing” or being “slash,” at least not in its strict sense!) It’s not even just about expanding one’s own career options and opportunities. It is mainly about being a professional in two or more areas and above all, the effort one is willing to make in order to level up or tap one’s potential. That is, by managing to develop two or more different specialized skills, one can literally go beyond oneself and lead a more fulfilling and rewarding life. Of course, this is no easy task, for having two areas of expertise simply means twice the hard work. However, experts often advise us to see it this way: while we’re making sacrifices to acquire another specialty, we’re also giving us an advantage over others. Experts have also pointed out that there are several requirements for a successful “slash” life. One is a thorough understanding of oneself. Only with an in-depth self-understanding can one realize which kinds of specialties (s)he wants to acquire. Having set a specific goal, one can then seek to develop sufficient enthusiasm and motivation, which are also key factors in a “slash” life. Then one can begin to make a plan, sharpen one’s time management skills, and get prepared to “slash.”
questions.explanation: 根據上述所言,下列何者非真? (B) 阿喜之所以正在「斜槓」,乃是因為拍戲比當工程 (A) 小華上網訂了 12:30 《冷案殺機》的電影票,所以 師佔去他更多時間。 他中午前就得抵達好萊塢影城。 (C) 阿喜在拍戲與當工程師間做出選擇, 成功做到 「斜 (B) 小建如果想觀賞 10:20 的《冷案殺機》,並且享用 槓」 。 些爆米花,總共得花 8 元。 (D) 阿喜正要發展他的第三專長,讓他的「斜槓」人 (C) 阿珠與阿花兩人花了 24 元,所以可以一面觀賞 生變得更豐富。 18:30 《蟲林》,一面享用熱狗與可樂。

options: “Slash” is a word originally from the local Chinese in Taiwan.

“Slashing” can be seen as an attitude ensuring a meaningful life.

an effective time table ▲ 閱讀下文,回答第 38-42 題 Kittie had just broken up with her fiancé, so she naturally felt lonely all the time. One day, she came upon a cute stray cat on the street. Out of sympathy as well as her desperate need for company, she decided to take it in, naming it Spotty. It turned out that spending time with Spotty did help Kittie get over her breakup. Whenever Spotty jumped into her arms or leaned gently against her, she felt so happy that she almost forgot that she’d been wounded badly a while before. Every night Spotty had dinner with Kittie. Later on, it would sleep right by her bedside. One Saturday morning, Kittie woke up from a sound sleep. Spotty, as usual, was sitting by her side and looking lovingly at her. However, a dead rat and a couple of roaches were lying next to it! Upon seeing them, Kittie began screaming and trembling with fear! Afterwards, she got baffled, wondering what had caused Spotty to do such a horrible thing to her. Before she took any “extreme measures,” such as giving Spotty a good beating, she went online and googled some interesting facts about cats. Soon she found the answers she was looking for: first, it is cats’ nature to hunt. In addition, once cats have become our pets, they tend to view their human owners as their family. And it’s also their nature to share the “results” of their hunts with their family. Therefore, their “gift-giving” behavior, as some people may call it, is actually a gesture of good will. (In Kittie’s case, she should feel happy because Spotty had just proved to her that it had officially seen her as its family!) Nowadays, more and more people argue that pets are not their owners’ property. They have their own rights and thus should be treated with respect. But respecting pets isn’t just giving them food and shelter, or even the most comfortable life their owners can think of. Instead, it is about managing to learn their ways. To be more specific, owners must make some effort to know what their pets are thinking and communicate with them. (For Kittie, the next thing she should do is to give Spotty a big hug and then secretly throw away its “gifts” for her.)
questions.explanation: 正解：(C)；來源：110-4-四技二專-00共同科目 英文解析.pdf
